verdict_reasoning: LYN is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the plasma membrane via N-myristoylation and palmitoylation of its N-terminal SH4 domain. It localizes stably to the plasma membrane (especially lipid rafts) in hematopoietic cells and associates directly with surface receptors (BCR, CD40, CD19). Although it has multiple subcellular pools (nucleus, endomembranes), the protein definitively reaches the outer leaflet via its own intrinsic covalent lipid modification mechanism, making the protein body surface-accessible for binder engagement.
